verses.text: The nations rage and kings conspire against the LORD and His Anointed, wishing to throw off His rule. Rebellion against God is as old as it is futile. Men still imagine that to cast off His law is freedom, when it is only revolt.

The nations rage and kings conspire against the LORD and His Anointed, wishing to throw off His rule. Rebellion against God is as old as it is futile. Men still imagine that to cast off His law is freedom, when it is only revolt.

The nations rage and kings conspire against the LORD and His Anointed, wishing to throw off His rule. Rebellion against God is as old as it is futile. Men still imagine that to cast off His law is freedom, when it is only revolt.

He who sits in heaven laughs at their plotting and declares, 'yet have I set my king upon my holy hill of Zion.' God is unshaken by human defiance. His purpose to enthrone His King stands firm, whatever the world may scheme.

He who sits in heaven laughs at their plotting and declares, 'yet have I set my king upon my holy hill of Zion.' God is unshaken by human defiance. His purpose to enthrone His King stands firm, whatever the world may scheme.

He who sits in heaven laughs at their plotting and declares, 'yet have I set my king upon my holy hill of Zion.' God is unshaken by human defiance. His purpose to enthrone His King stands firm, whatever the world may scheme.

The King proclaims the decree: 'Thou art my Son; this day have I begotten thee,' and is promised the nations for His inheritance. This is the eternal Son, Christ the Messiah. The Father gives Him the ends of the earth as His rightful possession.

The King proclaims the decree: 'Thou art my Son; this day have I begotten thee,' and is promised the nations for His inheritance. This is the eternal Son, Christ the Messiah. The Father gives Him the ends of the earth as His rightful possession.

The King proclaims the decree: 'Thou art my Son; this day have I begotten thee,' and is promised the nations for His inheritance. This is the eternal Son, Christ the Messiah. The Father gives Him the ends of the earth as His rightful possession.

Therefore the rulers are warned to serve the LORD with fear and 'Kiss the Son, lest he be angry,' for 'Blessed are all they that put their trust in him.' Submission to Christ is wisdom and safety. To trust the King the world rejects is the only sure refuge.
